decisions.rationale: IR re-read and code confirm DialogContent's built-in Radix close (dialog.tsx L45–48) overlaps the in-scope ai_RNbBHXkQ new-entry-close-btn (NewEntryPopup.tsx L131–144). Valid ui-fidelity gap, but AC-3 functional requirement—close dismisses without persist—passes and criteria_audit/ui_evidence mark ai_RNbBHXkQ pass. The same duplicate-close pattern appears across all journal popups (NewEntryPopup, EntryDetailPopup, AddMilestonePopup, EditMilestonePopup, LogRelapsePopup, EditRelapsePopup) sharing shadcn DialogContent; fixing via hideClose is a module-wide dialog-primitive polish, not a JOURNAL-03-only regression. Medium priority; no downstream depends_on item blocks on single-close parity. Defer to a journal popup dialog-shell polish wave (hideClose on DialogContent or journal-specific shell).

Finding is valid: NewEntryPopup accepts userId={user?.id ?? ""} (Journal.tsx L179) and handleSave does not short-circuit before setSaving(true). However Journal routes through ProtectedRoute/useRequireAuth, so an empty userId is unreachable in normal authenticated use; only a narrow auth-teardown race could surface the generic toast from createJournalEntry failure. Low priority; not in JOURNAL-03 ACs or ui_refs; same userId prop pattern is used for every journal popup on Journal.tsx. Disable-save when !userId is defensive hardening suitable for a later auth-edge-case polish wave, not a rewrite blocker for this gate.

summary: Review passed at 100% coverage with 0 blockers. Triage: 0 fix_now, 2 defer, 0 accept. Deferred: C-1 → journal popup dialog-shell polish (hideClose / single IR close btn across module popups); C-2 → optional auth-edge-case guard on Save when !userId (ProtectedRoute makes empty userId unreachable in practice). ok_to_advance=true, rewrite_required=false.
